all_candidates.topic: Can India build a foolproof public examination system now?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Prime Minister Narendra Modi called for a foolproof public examination system on July 21, 2026, following nationwide protests over recent paper leaks.

all_candidates.topic: Was the police action against CJP student protesters justified?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Police clashed with thousands of student protesters (CJP) marching to Parliament on July 20, 2026, demanding the Education Minister's resignation.

all_candidates.topic: Should infrastructure projects face stricter safety audits?
all_candidates.news_anchor: A Sikkim tunnel collapse on July 21, 2026, killed 10 workers and trapped others, raising questions about project safety.

all_candidates.topic: Should E20 petrol be mandated despite marginal mileage loss?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The government informed Parliament on July 20-21, 2026, that E20 petrol causes a marginal 3-5% drop in mileage for some older vehicles but no widespread engine damage.

all_candidates.topic: Does India's erratic monsoon demand new agricultural strategies?
all_candidates.news_anchor: A July 20, 2026, report stated that intensifying El Niño and global warming are causing more erratic monsoon patterns, threatening crops.

all_candidates.topic: Should FSSAI mandate digital traceability for all food products?
all_candidates.news_anchor: FSSAI asked the food industry on July 20, 2026, to strengthen quality assurance and adopt digital traceability amid rising consumer complaints on social media.

all_candidates.topic: Should FSSAI impose stricter penalties for misleading food claims?
all_candidates.news_anchor: FSSAI has stepped up enforcement against food businesses, including energy drink manufacturers, for alleged misleading claims and labeling, as reported on July 20, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Does India's varied weather demand hyper-local advisories?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The IMD on July 16, 2026, forecast persistent heatwave conditions in parts of Southern India while issuing heavy rain alerts for the North.

all_candidates.topic: Are IMD's heavy rainfall warnings adequate for public safety?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) issued warnings of extremely heavy rainfall across North India on July 21, 2026, due to active monsoon conditions.

all_candidates.topic: Should India offer specific support for H-1B returnees seeking jobs?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Reports on July 16, 2026, highlighted that thousands of H-1B visa holders returning to India face a challenging job market due to global tech layoffs.

all_candidates.topic: Will upgrading Mumbai local trains to 15 coaches ease congestion?
all_candidates.news_anchor: 10 Mumbai local train services were upgraded to 15 coaches from July 20, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Do new FCRA rules undermine NGO's freedom of association?
all_candidates.news_anchor: Amnesty International on July 19, 2026, urged India to repeal new Foreign Contribution (Regulation) Amendment Rules, citing concerns over NGO freedom.

all_candidates.topic: Will new railway projects truly boost local connectivity and economy?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Union Cabinet approved two railway projects worth Rs 3,907 crore in Odisha and Jharkhand on July 16, 2026, to add 145 km to the rail network.

all_candidates.topic: Is redeveloping 75 railway stations a good use of funds?
all_candidates.news_anchor: PM Modi is set to inaugurate 75 redeveloped railway stations and new railway projects, as reported around July 17, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Should India prioritize fully indigenous bullet train development?
all_candidates.news_anchor: A July 20, 2026, report discussed India and Japan jointly operating indigenous bullet trains on the Mumbai-Ahmedabad corridor.

all_candidates.topic: Should all digital platforms be mandated for accessibility compliance?
all_candidates.news_anchor: NCDEX issued compliance guidelines for digital accessibility under the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act on July 20, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Is India's first hydrogen train a viable step towards decarbonization?
all_candidates.news_anchor: India launched its first hydrogen-powered train on July 17, 2026, for an 89km route between Jind and Sonipat in Haryana.

all_candidates.topic: Should FSSAI strictly regulate minor seed oil extraction and use?
all_candidates.news_anchor: FSSAI proposed amendments on June 24, 2026, to regulate crude vegetable oils from minor seeds, with public comments due by July 25, 2026.

all_candidates.topic: Is the Sitamarhi-Raxaul railway doubling project truly beneficial?
all_candidates.news_anchor: RVNL secured a ₹358.97 crore contract on July 20, 2026, for the doubling of the Sitamarhi-Raxaul railway section in East Central Railway.

all_candidates.topic: Are new shipping pollution rules sufficient to protect coastal waters?
all_candidates.news_anchor: The Ministry of Ports, Shipping and Waterways notified Merchant Shipping (Prevention of Pollution by Sewage from Vessels) Rules, 2026, on July 20, 2026.
